title: Current Projects
text:
Dr. Jeshua Tromp
  
  
    Postdoctoral Researcher
    
      Donders Institute for Brain, Cognition and Behaviour
      Radboudumc
    
    Donders Institute, Radboudumc
    
    
      
      
      
      
      
      
    
  






Dynamics of the Gut-Brain Axis
The Gut-Brain Axis is a hot topic, with the (re)discovery of the importance of the microbes that live in our gut. Using large multimodal datasets, we want to explore the robustness of the links between what microbes we have in our gut and our brain and mental health. This project is embedded in the European Horizon Project, which aims to leverage large multimodal datasets to improve patient treatment, while also ensuring privacy compliance.


Brain Iron Metabolism
Iron is an essential mineral for our body to survive, but in excess it can also be toxic for our bodies. Here, I explore both the consequence of iron deficiency on essential brain processes, like synthesizing neurotransmitters, but also the detrimental effects of brain iron accumulation on cognition and brain health.


Neuromodulatory Underpinnings of the Variability of Cognitive Control
Why can we sometimes get into a good focus state, while other times we are distracted by even the smallest sound? In my dissertation, we researched the mechanistic underpinnings of this variability in attention and cognitive control by focusing on the neurotransmitter responsible for arousal: noradrenaline.

This website was built with Quarto, an open-source scientific publishing system.
The design of this website was inspired by the personal research website of Roberta Rosa Valtorta, whose clean and elegant academic layout served as a creative reference.
Thanks to The Academic Designer for highlighting great examples of scientific websites and providing inspiration for academics building their online presence.

title: Home
text:
Welcome!
I’m a cognitive neuroscientist, currently working as a postdoctoral research fellow at the Donders Institute in the beautiful city of Nijmegen. My current focus is on the relation between the gut-microbiome-brain axis, and uncovering the consequences of iron deficiency and iron overload on our brain, cognitive and mental health. Take a look at Projects to find out more about my current research projects. Also, feel free to reach out if you want to connect.
In my work, I use a combination of computational modeling and advanced statistical modeling, to uncover patterns in large multivariate datasets. Therefore, I’m coding a lot in R/Python, but mostly standing on the shoulders of the heroes that maintain research packages on Github. Beyond my direct research projects, I have a broad interest in everything science & philosophy! In my Dutch blog Hersenvoer, I write for a broad audience about my work and beyond, check it out!

A (very) short educational history
Just as my interests are broad, so was my formal education. First, you could find me mainly at the bench, pipetting and centrifuging to discover new drugs during my Bachelor’s Pharmaceutical Sciences at Utrecht University. After the course Psychopharmacology, I fell in love with Neuroscience, and decided to pursue a Master’s at Neuroscience & Cognition, also at Utrecht University and following that, a cognitive neuroscience PhD at Leiden University, where I tried to find the neuromodulatory underpinnings of why our focus seems so unstable.
